homepage.heroSubline: Quality repairs for Wauwatosa and Brookfield drivers. We handle everything from winter salt damage to collision repairs.
aboutText: Founded in 2012, Apex Auto Body has served Milwaukee-area drivers with honest, reliable auto body work. Our team grew from a two-bay shop in Walker's Point to a full-service facility.
We believe in transparent pricing and clear communication. You'll always know exactly what your car needs and why before we start work.
As locals ourselves, we understand how Milwaukee winters batter our cars. We're committed to keeping your vehicle safe through every season.

valueProps.title: Local Expertise
valueProps.description: We know exactly how Milwaukee weather affects vehicles year-round.

valueProps.title: Fair Pricing
valueProps.description: No hidden fees - just honest assessments and straightforward quotes.

valueProps.title: Quick Turnaround
valueProps.description: Most repairs completed within 3-5 business days.

valueProps.title: Lifetime Warranty
valueProps.description: We stand behind our work with a warranty on all repairs.

homepage.ctaSubline: Call 555-AUT-R92 or stop by our Milwaukee shop for a no-obligation assessment.

services.name: Collision Repair
services.shortDescription: Expert collision damage restoration using OEM procedures.
services.description:
From fender benders to major collisions, our Milwaukee technicians restore your vehicle's structure and appearance using manufacturer-approved methods. We start with a thorough damage assessment, checking for hidden structural issues common in Wisconsin winter accidents.

Our frame straightening equipment precisely restores your vehicle's alignment. We then match factory paint colors using computerized mixing systems, accounting for Milwaukee's harsh UV exposure that can fade finishes. Final quality checks ensure all safety systems function perfectly before you drive away.
process: Step 1: Comprehensive damage assessment with digital imaging
Step 2: Structural repairs using OEM specifications
Step 3: Precision paint matching and application
Step 4: Final safety inspection and road test

faq.question: How long does collision repair typically take?
faq.answer: Most repairs take 3-5 business days depending on damage severity. We'll provide a timeline after assessing your vehicle.

faq.question: Will my insurance cover the repairs?
faq.answer: We work with all major insurers and handle claims paperwork for you. Our estimates meet insurer requirements.

services.name: Rust Repair
services.shortDescription: Comprehensive rust treatment for Wisconsin vehicles.
services.description:
Milwaukee's heavy winter road salt takes a brutal toll on vehicles. Our rust repair services stop corrosion in its tracks and prevent future damage. We start by removing all affected metal, cutting back to clean material - no temporary patches.

For structural areas, we weld in new OEM-quality replacement panels. Non-structural areas receive corrosion-resistant treatments before priming and painting. We pay special attention to wheel wells, rocker panels, and undercarriage areas most vulnerable to Wisconsin's salt-heavy roads. The final step includes applying protective coatings to prevent future rust.
process: Step 1: Complete rust removal and surface preparation
Step 2: Replacement of severely corroded panels
Step 3: Application of rust-inhibiting primers
Step 4: Protective undercoating application
benefits: Prevents safety hazards from structural rust

faq.question: Can you prevent rust from coming back?
faq.answer: Our multi-layer protection system significantly slows future corrosion. Regular undercarriage washes in winter help maintain protection.

faq.question: Is rust repair covered by insurance?
faq.answer: Typically only if the rust resulted from a covered incident. We can assess your specific situation.

services.name: Paintless Dent Repair
services.shortDescription: Hail and dent removal without repainting.
services.description:
When Milwaukee's severe weather leaves dents but no paint damage, our paintless dent repair (PDR) techniques can often restore your vehicle's appearance without repainting. Using specialized tools, we massage dents out from behind the panel while preserving the factory finish.

This method works best on hail damage, parking lot dings, and minor impacts where the paint remains intact. We assess each dent individually - some may still require conventional repair. PDR typically takes just a few hours and costs significantly less than traditional bodywork while maintaining your car's original paint.
process: Step 1: Detailed dent assessment under proper lighting
Step 2: Accessing dent from behind using specialized tools
Step 3: Gradual reshaping of metal to original contours
Step 4: Final polish to restore factory finish

faq.question: What types of dents can be repaired this way?
faq.answer: PDR works best on shallow, rounded dents without paint damage. Sharp creases or cracked paint usually need conventional repair.

faq.question: Will the repair be noticeable?
faq.answer: Properly performed PDR leaves no visible signs of repair when done by our skilled technicians.

services.name: Frame Straightening
services.shortDescription: Precision frame repair using laser measurement.
services.description:
After a collision, your vehicle's frame may need precise realignment to ensure proper handling and safety. Our Milwaukee shop uses computerized laser measurement systems to restore your frame to factory specifications within millimeters.

This process is crucial for maintaining your car's structural integrity, especially important on Wisconsin's rough roads. We start with a complete diagnostic scan to identify all misalignments, then use hydraulic equipment to gradually return each point to its proper position. Final measurements confirm everything meets OEM tolerances before we proceed with other repairs.
process: Step 1: Complete frame measurement with laser alignment system
Step 2: Strategic application of hydraulic pressure to straighten
Step 3: Continuous measurement during straightening process
Step 4: Final verification of all reference points

faq.question: How do I know if my frame is bent?
faq.answer: Signs include uneven panel gaps, steering pull, or unusual tire wear. Our measurement system detects even minor misalignments.

faq.question: Is a straightened frame as strong as original?
faq.answer: When done properly using OEM procedures, the repaired frame maintains its designed strength and safety characteristics.

services.name: Paint Matching
services.shortDescription: Flawless color matching for seamless repairs.
services.description:
Milwaukee's seasonal temperature extremes can make paint matching challenging. Our technicians use advanced spectrophotometers to analyze your vehicle's current color, accounting for sun fading and environmental factors.

We then custom-mix paint to blend perfectly with your existing finish. The process includes testing sprayouts on sample panels under different lighting conditions to verify the match. For older vehicles, we may recommend blending into adjacent panels to ensure a seamless repair. Our Milwaukee shop maintains an extensive database of factory colors plus formulas for common fading patterns seen in our region.
process: Step 1: Color measurement with spectrophotometer
Step 2: Custom paint formulation accounting for age and exposure
Step 3: Test sprayout and lighting comparison
Step 4: Final application with proper clearcoat protection

faq.question: Why doesn't the paint match exactly sometimes?
faq.answer: Sun exposure changes paint over time. We adjust for this, but very old paint may require blending for perfect results.

faq.question: How long until the new paint fully hardens?
faq.answer: Paint cures completely in about 30 days. Avoid car washes and waxing during this period for best results.

services.name: Bumper Repair
services.description:
Bumpers take the brunt of Milwaukee's tight parking spots and winter driving conditions. We offer multiple repair options depending on damage severity. Minor scuffs often just need refinishing, while cracks may require plastic welding.

For modern vehicles with parking sensors and radar systems, we ensure all safety features remain fully functional after repair. Our technicians test each system before returning your vehicle. When replacement is necessary, we source OEM-quality parts that fit perfectly and maintain your car's designed impact protection.
process: Step 1: Damage assessment and repair plan
Step 2: Plastic welding or refinishing as needed
Step 3: Precise color matching and painting
Step 4: Sensor calibration and safety check
benefits: Quick turnaround for this common repair

faq.question: Can you repair cracked bumper covers?
faq.answer: Yes, we use specialized plastic welding techniques for many types of bumper damage.

faq.question: How long does bumper repair take?
faq.answer: Most bumper repairs are completed within 1-2 days depending on paint drying times.

services.name: Windshield Chip Repair
services.shortDescription: Affordable fix for small windshield damage.
services.description:
Milwaukee's gravel-strewn winter roads lead to countless windshield chips. When caught early, many chips can be repaired without full replacement. Our resin injection process fills the damage, preventing spreading and restoring structural integrity.

We evaluate each chip to ensure it meets repairable size and location criteria. The process takes about 30 minutes - much faster than replacement. While the repair won't be completely invisible, it prevents the damage from worsening and avoids the expense of a new windshield. We recommend addressing chips promptly before temperature changes cause them to spread.
process: Step 1: Chip cleaning and preparation
Step 2: Resin injection under vacuum pressure
Step 3: UV curing for maximum strength
Step 4: Final polish and inspection
benefits: No need for replacement in many cases

faq.question: What types of chips can be repaired?
faq.answer: Most star-shaped chips and bullseyes smaller than a quarter can be repaired if not in the driver's direct line of sight.

faq.question: Does insurance cover chip repair?
faq.answer: Many Wisconsin policies cover chip repair with no deductible since it prevents more expensive replacement.

reviews.name: Mark T.
reviews.text: Apex saved my 2015 Silverado from Wisconsin winters. They cut out all the rust in my wheel wells and replaced it properly - not just covering it up like other shops tried. Six months later and the repairs still look perfect.

reviews.name: Sarah K.
reviews.text: After hitting a deer on Highway 100, Apex had my Camry looking brand new. They found hidden frame damage others missed and worked directly with my insurance. The paint match on my pearl white is flawless.

reviews.name: James P.
reviews.text: The hail storm last June left my Accord looking like a golf ball. Apex removed all 37 dents without repainting - you can't tell it ever happened. They even helped with my insurance claim paperwork.

reviews.name: Lisa M.
reviews.text: Backed into a pole in the Sendik's parking lot - embarrassing but Apex made it right. They plastic-welded my cracked bumper and matched the metallic gray perfectly. Total was half what the dealer wanted for replacement.

reviews.name: David R.
reviews.text: Got a nasty chip driving on I-94 near the zoo interchange. Apex fixed it same-day and prevented what would've been a full windshield replacement come winter. The tech explained exactly how the resin works to stop spreading.

reviews.name: Jennifer S.
reviews.text: Had rust spots repaired on my red Jeep and was worried about color match. Apex nailed it - even accounting for the fading from parking at my Bay View apartment. The blend line is invisible.

With harsh winters bringing heavy lake-effect snow, your vehicle is susceptible to road salt damage and winter collisions—trust our expert team to restore your car to pristine condition. Whether you're navigating the charming streets lined with 1920s-40s Tudor and Colonial homes or commuting to downtown Milwaukee, we ensure your vehicle stays safe and reliable year-round.
